mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts are followed by positive 20-day returns and fails if easing does not improve equity performance relative to unchanged-rate periods.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or below baseline in periods where FEDFUNDS is falling, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises above recent levels, equity valuations reprice downward as the short rate anchors the discount curve and tightens liquidity. The effect should appear within one month and is invalidated if rate hikes are not followed by weaker forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.50 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered hikes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if average post-trigger returns are not materially below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A falling UNRATE reflects labor-market improvement that supports household income expectations and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a one-quarter horizon. The effect is falsified if declining unemployment does not coincide with stronger forward equity performance.
failure_condition: Rejected if 60-day forward SPX returns are not higher when UNRATE trends down over the prior 3 months, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration that raises recession risk and compresses equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is confirmed when unemployment jumps and forward returns weaken; it fails if unemployment spikes are not followed by equity drawdowns.
failure_condition: Rejected if more than 40% of triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns, or if triggered returns are not significantly below non-triggered returns.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening payroll income and retail demand, which supports near-term earnings expectations and positive equity returns. The mechanism is not decomposable into either series alone because both labor income and consumer spending must improve together to validate the signal.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint-high PAYEMS/RSAFS condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in the top interaction quartile.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in INDPRO together with stable or falling CPIAUCSL signals a positive supply impulse: output is expanding without renewed inflation pressure, which supports margins and lowers rate pressure on equities. This mechanism should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month and fails if industrial expansion occurs alongside accelerating inflation.
failure_condition: Rejected if periods with rising INDPRO and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL do not show above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction effect disappears in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls sharply, it signals a cooling housing cycle that tends to weaken cyclically sensitive demand and forward equity returns over the next quarter. The effect is expected to be strongest after a clear downside break in housing starts and is falsified if housing weakness does not precede equity softness.
failure_condition: Rejected if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative on average after the housing decline trigger, or if triggered episodes produce positive returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Weak growth in GDPC1 combined with elevated UNRATE identifies a recessionary demand shock that should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal requires both slower output and labor deterioration; it fails if one series moves adversely without the other and equities do not weaken.
failure_condition: Rejected if the combined low-GDPC1-growth and high-UNRATE condition does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
